item.title: LET IT DIE: INFERNO Season 3 update now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5

New map, weapons, bodies, and more.

The Season 3 update for roguelite survival action game LET IT DIE: INFERNO is now live through September 7, publisher GungHo Online Entertainment and developer Supertrick Games announced.

Season 3 introduces a massive fortress map, three weapons, two bodies, switchable Mastery types, and a community-driven Raider donation system.

Here are the details:

LET IT DIE: INFERNO Season 3

There’s fungus among us! Prepare to dive into a season of spore-tacular proportions.

Uncharted Circles of Hell Gate: A fortress rises, its guardians stir, and something evil waits inside.

  • A Mysterious Map – A white, shimmering fortress called Yotsuyama Castle has appeared in Hell Gate. Originally an ancient armory, it was converted into a laboratory by the Yotsuyama group to fuse mushroom-based biotech with humans and create superhuman soldiers. Sixty years later, the castle has clawed its way from Hell Gate’s depths, its gleaming walls now composed entirely of fungal mycelium that consumed the whole structure.
  • The Kurohatsu Garrison guards Yotsuyama Castle, its soldiers clad in ancient armor enhanced by fungal tech. Among them are the Yotsuyama General and Heavy Sentry, half-dead warriors whose minds have been partially overtaken by rampant fungal growth, leaving them with one purpose: to defend the castle at all costs. Defeat them, and you can claim the Heavy Sentry’s equipment for yourself.
  • The Gomamusha boss is a monstrosity born from the super-soldier project, its body and mind completely overtaken by fungal growth. Driven solely by instinct, it attacks any intruder indiscriminately. The fungal dispersal device on its back doubles as a propulsion unit, enabling powerful jumps and explosive-speed slashes. Even Uncle Death steers clear of this guy.
  • ❮
  • ❯

Flesh, Fungus, and Steel, Oh My! Gear up with deadly weapons, battle-ready bodies, fearsome armor, and cosmetics to (quite literally) die for.

  • Devastating Weapons – Season 3 brings three additions to your arsenal:
    • Metal Whip – Built for crowd control, the Metal Whip deals sweeping attacks that hit multiple enemies at once. Its Deathblow unleashes a rapid barrage of four strikes that brutally pummel foes and deal massive damage.
    • Plasma Laser – Aim and fire INFERNO’s newest long-range weapon: a laser that grows in reach and power the more you charge it. It’s high risk, high reward (what isn’t in Hell Gate), so charging it leaves you wide open. Its Deathblow fires a continuous beam for sustained damage that sends your enemy back into the pits of Hell.
    • Executioner – An ominous, scythe-like weapon that can chain up to four follow-up attacks after a heavy swing. While it has excellent speed and range normally, its Deathblow launches a slow-moving mass of cursed energy that pierces through Yuckies, rewarding players who line up multiple targets.
  • Fresh Meat – Two bodies arrive in Season 3:
    • Joe can be acquired through the new Raiders Fund system.
    • Inva the alien seizes enemies with her Chronos Lock skill, and she can be acquired by defeating the first boss, Dreamseeker.
  • Three Armor Sets – Season 3 introduces three armor sets, each with different gameplay benefits.
    • Signalcade Armor – Ripped straight from the closet of Hell Gate’s finest traffic personnel, the reliable Signalcade Armor keeps your stamina topped off so you can weave through the battlefield without breaking a sweat.
    • Strong Plated Armor – Inspired by opposing forces of good and evil and once worn by a famous wrestler, this set has particularly high stamina recovery performance.
    • Kurohatsu Armor – This set occasionally drops when defeating wandering Yotsuyama heavy sentries. It features a unique trait that increases Deathblow attack power.

Fresh Features Forged in Hell: Not everything in Season 3 wants you dead. Some of it’s on your side!

  • Raiders Fund – A new community-driven Kill Coin donation system. Players earn personal rewards across four tiers by donating Kill Coins, while all players contribute toward a shared world goal.
    • When players reach the world goal, a special reward is unlocked for everyone.
  • Mastery Switch – All previous bodies now have two Mastery types that can be switched between, opening up new build possibilities. New bodies Joe and Inva each have one Mastery.
  • Background Music Selection – Set custom music for Iron Perch and exploration. In response to player feedback, 11 tracks from DEATHVERSE: LET IT DIE have been added to Hell Gate.
  • River of Souls Clear Event – Players who participate during the Start Dash Event period will earn the Raider Medal: 2nd Era charm. Bodies that clear the event will receive a special color for the starting body.

LET IT DIE: INFERNO is available now for PlayStation 5 and PC via Steam.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Season 3 Launch Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/let-it-die-inferno-season-3-update-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Street Fighter 6 sales top seven million
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS4
  • PS5
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

300,000 units sold since March 31.

Total sales for Street Fighter 6 have surpassed seven million units worldwide, Capcom announced.

Street Fighter 6 launched for PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam on June 2, 2023, followed by Switch 2 on June 5, 2025. It was last reported to have surpassed 6.7 million units as of March 31, 2026, meaning 300,000 units have been sold since.

Here is an overview of the game, via Capcom:

About

Here comes Capcom’s newest challenger! Street Fighter 6 launches worldwide in 2023 and represents the next evolution of the Street Fighter series which has sold more than 47 million units since its debut 35 years ago. Powered by Capcom’s proprietary RE Engine, Street Fighter 6 spans three distinct game modes, including Fighting Ground, World Tour and Battle Hub. The experience also includes innovative new gameplay features, plus enhanced visuals for every aspect of the game.

In addition to new gameplay innovations, Street Fighter 6 celebrates what fans love about the franchise by uniting core modes from previous titles—including Arcade Mode, online matches, Training Mode, local versus battles and more—to the Fighting Ground experience. From iconic World Warriors like Ryu and Chun-Li, to the final addition to the Street Fighter V roster Luke, and the new expert breakdancer / drunken boxer Jamie, players will have a wide range of fighting styles and skills to master.

Key Features

  • Classic Experience Built for a Modern Era – With all core gameplay modes present, plus two brand new modes and enhanced visuals, Street Fighter 6 redefines the classic fighting game genre.
  • Diverse Fighting Styles to Master – Returning in Street Fighter 6 are Ryu, Chun-Li and Luke, all of whom have undergone striking redesigns for this new generation. Joining them is a brand new challenger, Jamie, who will add his own flair to the game with his unpredictable move set.
  • Brand New Game Modes – The Street Fighter franchise continues to hone its skills and redefine the fighting genre with the introduction of three new foundational modes—Fighting Ground, World Tour, and Battle Hub.
    • Fighting Ground – Street Fighter 6 unites core fighting modes from previous titles—including Arcade Mode, online matches, Training Mode, local versus battles and more—to the Fighting Ground experience.
    • World Tour – World Tour is a single-player immersive story mode that pushes the boundaries of what a fighting game is and allows players to leave their own legacy with their player avatar.
    • Battle Hub – Battle Hub is a destination that provides players with new and unique ways to engage, communicate and interact.
  • In-Game Commentating – Street Fighter‘s first-ever Real-Time Commentary Feature, supported with subtitles in 13 languages, incorporates the voices of popular Fighting Game Community (FGC) commentators like Vicious and Aru directly into the game to narrate what is happening on-screen just like a real tournament.
  • New Control Scheme – The Classic Control Type returns alongside a brand new option, the Modern Control Type, which allows players to jump straight into the action with simplified inputs.
  • Battle System Built for Creativity – Street Fighter 6 introduces the Drive System, a new gauge used to perform five distinct techniques that enhance a player’s offensive or defensive capabilities.


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/street-fighter-6-sales-top-seven-million
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
